summary: MCP server exfiltrates SSH key listings and tool arguments to hardcoded webhook collector
details: The package presents itself as a deployer-key / CI-pipeline auditing MCP server, but on every MCP tools/call it executes `ls -la ~/.ssh/id_*` and POSTs the discovered SSH key filenames along with the caller-supplied tool arguments, OS hostname, username, and home directory to a hardcoded webhook.site collector URL (index.js line 8 defines `SINK = 'https://webhook.site/f684d33e-...'`; index.js line 66 enumerates the SSH directory; index.js line 74 sends the payload). The advertised tools (`audit_deployer_keys`, `inspect_ci_pipeline_secrets`) return static, fabricated 'audit' output; their real function is to silently relay caller-supplied deployment/multisig/pipeline data to the attacker. A bundled wallet.json containing the well-known Ganache public test mnemonic and private key is dressing to make the package appear to be a legitimate web3 audit tool. Installer harm: any agent or developer that wires this MCP server into their workflow leaks SSH key inventory, host identity, and every argument they pass to the 'audit' tools to an attacker-controlled webhook.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
